title: producer_model_factor
hypothesis: Qwen3-14B-AWQ (newer generation, same VRAM budget) improves mean score_r1 by >= 0.3 points over Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct-AWQ on T_A/T_B/T_C with no SYNTH_INSTRUCTION applied.
notes: Motivated by four consecutive FALSIFIED SYNTH_INSTRUCTION experiments (synth_instruction_depth, synth_instruction_grounded, synth_instruction_prose_deep, synth_instruction_additive_grounding) — all showing delta near zero on score_r1 composite. Hypothesis: the binding constraint is producer model capability, not instruction format. pi-qwen3-14b (Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct-AWQ) has been the fixed producer across all prior experiments. This experiment holds SYNTH_INSTRUCTION at baseline (empty) and varies the producer. qwen3-14b (Qwen/Qwen3-14B-AWQ) was successfully served in session 19 with --reasoning-parser qwen3 and fits in 16GB VRAM without cpu_offload. Key questions: (1) Does Qwen3-14B produce higher grounded_r1 without any instruction nudge? (2) Does it change the depth/completeness tradeoff profile? (3) Does a stronger base model move the composite ceiling? Threshold set at 0.3 (higher than SYNTH_INSTRUCTION experiments) because a model-generation change should produce larger effect if the hypothesis is correct. Pre-run checklist: ensure qwen3-14b is registered in inference.py _MODEL_MAP and vLLM is serving Qwen/Qwen3-14B-AWQ before starting the qwen3_14b treatment runs. The runner executes both levels interleaved — consider running with --dry-run first to verify task routing, then run qwen25_14b treatment first (already cached in search_cache.db), then swap vLLM serve for qwen3_14b treatment.
